You know that very popular story of the 502 experiments and what would I be doing if I hadn't discovered electricity. Now I'd be doing experiment number 503. Nobody really thinks about the fact that if it took you 503 times to figure this thing out, you know, how smart are you? You know, gee, couldn't you get there quicker if you're the world's greatest inventor? Interestingly, five would have done it, you know. The reputation was grossly exaggerated as an inventor in part by his own deliberate exaggeration. What he was was a fabulous promoter of himself and very good at getting money, very good at getting money from investors. Lots and lots and lots of money for thousands and thousands and thousands of inventions that never came to market. The interesting thing out of this little block copy is that in his demonstrations he often talked about fascinating applications which he actually had no intent of spending any time or money on related to the invention that he was promoting. Well, why? Well because to invent this thing that increases the current and the wall socket and makes it, but if this thing causes robots to run around and bake your milkshakes for you, well that's pretty sexy and exciting, so let's talk about robots. Even though we have no intention of ever making a robot, we'll talk about robots. He was pretty unabashed about that through his entire career. Andrew Carnegie has this reputation carefully cultivated at the time of being the world's greatest industrialist. Well what he was better at was raising money. And Carnegie spent most of his time shuttling back and forth between the United States and Europe getting money. That's really what he was really good at, is putting 10 people in a room and pitching up. That he was a master at. Again, Trump, people think he's in the real estate business, he's not in the real estate business, he's in the Trump business. So what you see is rarely what is. We think is their business rarely is their business. And the same is true with us. So it is a mistake to think about this business. Has a coaching business or a consulting business or an information business or an advisory business. It is not. It is a self aggrandizement business. It is a promotion business. It is a client control business, you know, etc. Over to, well let's go ahead and do it, page 58. It's kind of interesting. I'm always suspicious of academic research unless I agree with it. But it's always nice to have legitimate academic research when you do agree with it. Here's what they did. So they take two job candidates, they're all the same, right? Except one of them. The letter says sometimes John can be difficult to get along with. And then hiring directors had a choice of interviewing either one of the two people. Which do you think they took? Yeah, the answer is kind of obvious given the context of our conversation, right? Is that the guy who was billed as sometimes difficult to get along with was asked to be interviewed much more than the guy who did not have that criticism in his references. Why? Well, we can speculate about why, but the best likelihood is exactly what they concluded that it made John a more believable candidate because the reference not only said all the good things about him, but it said a bad thing about him. So again, now let's think of what is being said about Sharon Stone's character by Jeff Bridges. And so what do you want to control? What is being said about you to other people, by the people in your coaching group, by your clients? What reputation is constructed and spread from person to person to person to person? What do you want it to be? So mostly what I want said is that I'm difficult to do business with and I'm hard to get to. So I want them saying, I want them saying all the good stuff, right? Kennedy's a genius. He knows more about marketing seminars than anybody on the planet. He made me $12 million last year. He'll make you a fortune. If you could, well, it's a line from the movie, if you can get to see her, it could change your life. I just want the butt attached to it. But you should know he's a real pain in the ass to deal with and you can never get him on a damn phone and he's irritable. And if you aggravate him, he won't work with you and he's kind of difficult to deal with. That's what I want said.
